  • Element
Package org.apache.juneau.marshall

Annotation Interface MarshalledConfig

@Target({TYPE,METHOD}) @Retention(RUNTIME) @Inherited @ContextApply(Applier.class) public @interface MarshalledConfig
Annotation for specifying marshalling-only config properties defined in MarshallingContext and MarshallingTraverseContext.

Used primarily for specifying marshalling configuration properties on REST classes and methods.

This annotation is the marshalling-only sibling of @BeanConfig. Bean-modeling attributes (visibility settings, fluent-setter detection, property naming, not-bean exclusions, etc.) live on @BeanConfig in juneau-commons. Attributes that affect wire format, type discriminators, swap classes, locale/media-type/timezone, and debug mode stay here on @MarshalledConfig.

  • Element Details

    • debug

      String debug
      Debug mode.

      Enables the following additional information during serialization:

      • When bean getters throws exceptions, the exception includes the object stack information in order to determine how that method was invoked.
      • Enables MarshallingTraverseContext.Builder.detectRecursions().

      Enables the following additional information during parsing:

      • When bean setters throws exceptions, the exception includes the object stack information in order to determine how that method was invoked.
      • "true"
      • "false" (default)
      Notes:
      • Supports VarResolver.DEFAULT (e.g. "$C{myConfigVar}").
      See Also:
      • Context.Builder.debug()
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • dictionary

      Class<?>[] dictionary
      Bean dictionary.

      The list of classes that make up the bean dictionary in this bean context.

      A dictionary is a name/class mapping used to find class types during parsing when they cannot be inferred through reflection.
      The names are defined through the @Marshalled(typeName) annotation defined on the bean class.
      For example, if a class Foo has a type-name of "myfoo", then it would end up serialized as "{_type:'myfoo',...}".

      This setting tells the parsers which classes to look for when resolving "_type" attributes.

      See Also:
      • Marshalled.dictionary()
      • MarshalledProp.dictionary()
      • dictionary_replace()
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.beanDictionary(Class...)
      • Bean Dictionary Basics
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • dictionary_replace

      Class<?>[] dictionary_replace
      Replace bean dictionary.

      Same as dictionary() but replaces any existing value.

      See Also:
      • Marshalled.dictionary()
      • MarshalledProp.dictionary()
      • dictionary()
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.beanDictionary(Class...)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • locale

      String locale
      Locale.

      Specifies the default locale for serializer and parser sessions.

      Notes:
      • Supports VarResolver.DEFAULT (e.g. "$C{myConfigVar}").
      See Also:
      • MarshallingSession.Builder.locale(Locale)
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.locale(Locale)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • mediaType

      String mediaType
      Media type.

      Specifies the default media type value for serializer and parser sessions.

      Notes:
      • Supports VarResolver.DEFAULT (e.g. "$C{myConfigVar}").
      See Also:
      • MarshallingSession.Builder.mediaType(MediaType)
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.mediaType(MediaType)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • rank

      int rank
      Optional rank for this config.

      Can be used to override default ordering and application of config annotations.

      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      0

    • swaps

      Class<?>[] swaps
      Java object swaps.

      Swaps are used to "swap out" non-serializable classes with serializable equivalents during serialization, and "swap in" the non-serializable class during parsing.

      An example of a swap would be a Calendar object that gets swapped out for an ISO8601 string.

      Multiple swaps can be associated with a single class.
      When multiple swaps are applicable to the same class, the media type pattern defined by ObjectSwap.forMediaTypes() or @Swap(mediaTypes) are used to come up with the best match.

      See Also:
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.swaps(Class...)
      • Swap Basics
      • Per-media-type Swaps
      • One-way Swaps
      • @Swap Annotation
      • Auto-detected swaps
      • Surrogate Classes
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

    • swaps_replace

      Class<?>[] swaps_replace
      Replace Java object swap classes.

      Same as swaps() but replaces any existing value.

      See Also:
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.swaps(Class...)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      {}

      Calendar wire format.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      NOT_SET

      BooleanFormat booleanFormat

      ClassFormat classFormat
      Class wire format for Class values.
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      NOT_SET

    • timeZone

      String timeZone
      Time zone.

      Specifies the default timezone for serializer and parser sessions.

      Notes:
      • Supports VarResolver.DEFAULT (e.g. "$C{myConfigVar}").
      See Also:
      • MarshallingSession.Builder.timeZone(TimeZone)
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.timeZone(TimeZone)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""

    • typePropertyName

      String typePropertyName
      Bean type property name.

      This specifies the name of the bean property used to store the dictionary name of a bean type so that the parser knows the data type to reconstruct.

      Notes:
      • Default value: "_type".
      • Supports VarResolver.DEFAULT (e.g. "$C{myConfigVar}").
      See Also:
      • Marshalled.typePropertyName()
      • MarshallingContext.Builder.typePropertyName(String)
      Returns:
      The annotation value.
      Default:
      ""
